seekers.name: Jane Smith
seekers.headline: Senior Application Security Engineer
seekers.skills: Application Security, OWASP Top 10, Penetration Testing, Python, JavaScript, React, Node.js, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, SAST/DAST, Threat Modeling, Security Code Review
seekers.about:
## About Me

I'm a passionate Application Security Engineer with 8 years of experience securing web applications and cloud infrastructure. I specialize in integrating security into the SDLC, conducting security assessments, and building security automation tools.

I'm deeply involved in the OWASP community and have contributed to several projects including OWASP ZAP and the OWASP Top 10. I believe in making security accessible and practical for development teams.

When I'm not breaking things (professionally), I enjoy contributing to open source, writing technical blog posts, and speaking at security conferences.

## Work Experience

**Senior Application Security Engineer** at **TechCorp Inc** (Jan 2022 - Present)
- Lead security assessments for 20+ microservices and web applications
- Implemented automated security testing in CI/CD pipeline, reducing vulnerabilities by 65%
- Designed and delivered security training program for 100+ developers
- Built internal security tools using Python and Go for vulnerability management
- Conducted threat modeling sessions for new product features

**Application Security Engineer** at **SecureStart** (Mar 2019 - Dec 2021)
- Performed security code reviews and penetration testing for client applications
- Developed SAST/DAST integration for multiple CI/CD platforms
- Created security documentation and secure coding guidelines
- Mentored junior security engineers and interns

**Security Analyst** at **CyberDefense LLC** (Jun 2016 - Feb 2019)
- Conducted vulnerability assessments and security audits
- Responded to security incidents and performed forensic analysis
- Implemented security monitoring and alerting systems
- Collaborated with development teams on security remediation

## Projects & Portfolio

- **SecureCI** - Open source security scanning orchestrator for CI/CD pipelines [GitHub](https://github.com/janesmith/secureci) | [Docs](https://secureci.dev)
- **OWASP ZAP** - Core contributor, added 15+ scanning rules and improved API security testing capabilities
- **VulnTracker** - Vulnerability management dashboard built with React and Node.js [Demo](https://vulntracker-demo.com)
- **Security Blog** - Technical blog with 50+ articles on application security [Blog](https://janesmith.dev/blog)

## Certifications & Education

- OSCP (Offensive Security Certified Professional) - 2023
- CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional) - 2021
- CEH (Certified Ethical Hacker) - 2019
- AWS Certified Security - Specialty - 2022
- M.S. Cybersecurity, Stanford University - 2018
- B.S. Computer Science, MIT - 2016

## Work Samples

- Blog: "Securing React Applications: A Comprehensive Guide" - https://janesmith.dev/blog/securing-react-apps
- Talk: "Building Security into DevOps" at DEF CON 30 - https://youtube.com/watch?v=example
- Research: "Novel Techniques for Detecting XSS in Modern SPAs" - https://arxiv.org/example
- Podcast: "The Security Engineer's Mindset" on Security Weekly - https://securityweekly.com/jane-smith
- Workshop: "Hands-on Web Application Security" at OWASP Global AppSec - https://github.com/janesmith/appsec-workshop

## What I'm Looking For

I'm seeking a Senior or Lead Application Security Engineer role at a company that values security as a core principle, not an afterthought. Ideal opportunities would involve:

- Building and leading security programs from the ground up
- Working with modern cloud-native architectures (Kubernetes, serverless, etc.)
- Collaborating with engineering teams to build security into the development process
- Contributing to open source security tools and projects
- Mentoring and growing a security team

I'm particularly interested in companies in the fintech, healthcare, or developer tools space. Remote work is preferred, but I'm open to hybrid arrangements for the right opportunity.

Salary expectations: $180k - $220k depending on location and benefits.

## About Me

<!-- Brief professional summary/bio (2-3 paragraphs recommended) -->
I am a software developer with a strong focus on Python and backend engineering, currently diving deep into system design and agentic workflows. I'm actively involved in the open-source community, where I enjoy building optimized, high-impact tools that bridge the gap between complex architecture and practical execution.

Actively contributing to real-world, open-source codebases is a powerful catalyst for sustained technical growth. Engaging with live projects on the internet transforms theoretical learning into applied, result-driven execution. This dual-impact approach ensures that while you are continuously expanding your own engineering capabilities, you are simultaneously delivering tangible optimizations that improve the functionality and reliability of existing software.

- Developed and integrated synchronous state management for real-time collaboration, delivering granular audio/video controls, synchronized participant interactions (raise-hand functionality), and optimized lobby presence interfaces.
